title: English Mastery & Application
description: Spoken English, Idioms, IELTS strategies, and Business communication tips.

rules: Stop translating from Hindi. Direct English thought leads to direct English speech.
Use filler words (Well..., Actually..., Honestly...) to buy thinking time naturally.
Avoid single-word answers; expand your response to maintain conversation flow.

examples.english: Well, I think English is very important nowadays.
examples.hindi: खैर, मुझे लगता है कि आजकल अंग्रेज़ी बहुत महत्वपूर्ण है।

rules: Native English relies heavily on idioms and phrasal verbs rather than literal words.

examples.english: Hit the nail on the head.

rules: IELTS speaking tests natural fluency, not memorization. Use the Idea + Reason + Example + Conclusion structure.
International English demands clear pronunciation and neutral accents rather than faking British or American accents.

examples.english: I enjoy reading books because they improve my knowledge. For example, I recently read a science fiction novel.
examples.hindi: मुझे किताबें पढ़ना पसंद है क्योंकि ये मेरा ज्ञान बढ़ाती हैं।

rules: Focus on professional communication, business emails, and negotiation English.
Be polite but direct in meetings and corporate presentations.

examples.english: Please find attached the report for your review.
examples.hindi: कृपया समीक्षा के लिए संलग्न रिपोर्ट देखें।
